NABARD Hackathon @ GFF 2026 Launch Demo
NABARD Hackathon @ GFF 2026

The bank sees a healthy business.
Drishti sees what happens next.

63 million rural micro enterprises power India's economy but remain invisible to the financial system. Drishti scores cash flow risk using 50+ explainable rules — tracing debt across 5 layers the bank never sees.

Sectors mapped

50+

Rulebook rules

0

Black boxes

63M enterprises. 479 DDMs.

That's ~131,500 enterprises per district. At 8 visits per day, 250 working days a year — it takes ~66 years to visit each enterprise once.

Remote risk monitoring isn't optional. It's the only way.

63M

Rural micro enterprises

NABARD structure

To visit each once

At 8 visits/day

The Pattern

Enterprise health ≠ household health

Across 63M rural enterprises, the same pattern repeats: the business looks healthy on paper, but 5 layers of hidden household debt tell a different story. Drishti sees both.

Enterprise Layer

Healthy

₹67K

Avg revenue/mo

15x

Enterprise DSCR

₹35K

Savings

Revenue ₹67K avg, EMI ₹4.5K — enterprise-only DSCR is 15x. Looks great on paper.

But look deeper

Household Layer

Debt Trap
Formal EMI ₹4,500
SHG internal lending ₹2,500
Feed supplier credit ₹3,000
Moneylender (spouse treatment) ₹2,000
True disposable income ₹10K/mo NEGATIVE

Husband stopped earning (back injury). 4 dependents, 1 earner. ₹12K/mo debt from 4 hidden sources.

Savings Floor

39 days until completely broke

₹35K

covers 1.3 months

What Drishti Sees

50

Risk Score (0–100)

Yellow — watch condition

Enterprise DSCR 15x (healthy)
Household DSCR 0.7x (critical)
Cash Runway 1.3 months
Savings Floor Caution (2.9x EMI)

Household trap detected: 5-layer debt stacking consumes ₹12K/mo from ₹8K income. Enterprise-only DSCR is 15x — but household DSCR craters to 0.7x. One medical bill from distress.

Action: Build 3-month savings buffer via SHG weekly commitment (₹2,500/mo). Restructure informal loan through BRC. Estimated ₹5K/mo savings from consolidation.

The Engine

7 components. 50+ rules. Every number explained.

Each enterprise is scored across 7 risk components. Rules are configurable per sector — dairy weighs feed costs more heavily, retail weighs udhaar recovery. No black boxes.

25%

Cash Runway

Months until savings are depleted. The single most important number for a credit officer.

20%

DSCR

Debt Service Coverage Ratio — uses the WORSE of enterprise DSCR and household DSCR. Indian banks require ≥1.25x.

20%

Income Stability

Coefficient of Variation of monthly income — consistency matters more than volume.

10%

Seasonal Position

Sector-specific peak and trough periods. Dairy in May (lean) vs November (flush) are completely different risk profiles.

10%

Input Cost Pressure

Feed price spikes, disease outbreaks, monsoon disruptions. Dairy-weighted 1.5x — feed is 60-70% of cost.

10%

Recovery Quality

Udhaar recovery rate. For kirana stores, 40-90% of revenue is credit — defaults destroy cash flow. Retail-weighted 1.8x.

5%

External Risk

Dynamic CV-based market competition risk. Context signal, not primary driver.

Sector-Specific Weight Adjustments

🐄

Dairy

Input cost ×1.5

🏪

Retail

Recovery ×1.8

🐔

Poultry

Input cost ×1.3

👷

MGNREGA

Seasonal ×1.5

🌾

Agriculture

Seasonal ×1.3

What's Different

Three things other systems miss

Traditional credit scoring sees the enterprise. Drishti sees the household, the peer group, and the reasoning behind every number.

Enterprise ≠ Household

The bank sees the enterprise. Drishti traces 5 layers of household debt the bank never sees: formal, SHG internal, trader credit, cooperative, and informal moneylenders.

Savings Floor: The single best predictor of microfinance default. Shows "days until broke" — the number that matters most.

Seasonal ≠ Individual

"Is everyone struggling, or just you?" Compares enterprise to district sector peers. Distinguishes seasonal trough from individual distress.

Red + low percentile = individual distress
Amber + seasonal = expected recovery month
At peer level = monitor, don't intervene

Every number has a reason

50+ configurable rules, each with research provenance. A DDM can trace any flag back to its source data. A NABARD economist can adjust thresholds without touching code.

Confidence layers: verified (external signals), reported (enterprise data), estimated (household context). Divergence between layers is more informative than the composite score.

DPI Integration

Digital Public Infrastructure signals

Drishti integrates with India's DPI stack when available. When signals are missing, the engine works with what it has — no data, no guess.

NACH Bounce Rate

SIMULATED

Probability-estimated from DSCR. Strongest single repayment signal.

UPI Transaction Velocity

SIMULATED

7-day vs 30-day ratio with sector-specific penetration rates (dairy 30%, retail 45%).

MGNREGA Demand

SIMULATED

Block-level person-day spike as context signal. Max +10 risk — correlation is weak (r=0.3).

Mandi Prices

SIMULATED

Input cost vs MSP ratio with seasonal adjustment. Stress if price >15% above baseline.

All 4 signals are simulated for demo. Production deployment would connect to NPCI, NREGASoft, and Agmarknet APIs.

Who It's For

Built for two audiences

The DDM who can't visit 131,500 enterprises. The economist who can't wait 3 months for a developer.

For 479 DDMs across India

  • See all enterprises in your district, color-coded by risk level
  • Drill down into 5-layer household debt the bank never sees
  • Distinguish seasonal trough from individual distress via peer comparison
  • Every score has a full explanation — trace any flag to source data

For NABARD economists & policy designers

  • Every threshold is a configurable rule — change a feed cost trigger without touching code
  • Sector-specific weights — dairy emphasizes input costs, retail emphasizes udhaar recovery
  • Research provenance — every rule cites its source (CGAP, NAFIS, industry practice)
  • Version tracking — see what changed, when, why, and what's due for review

Sectors

Built for rural India's real economy

Each sector has unique seasonal rhythms, cost structures, and risk factors. Drishti maps all five.

🐄

Dairy

₹84K–2L/mo · Flush/lean cycles

Pain: Feed cost 60-70%, heat stress, cooperative payment delays

🏪

Rural Retail

₹1–3L/mo · Festival demand cycles

Pain: Udhaar trap (40-90% revenue as credit), thin margins

🐔

Poultry

₹1.5–5L/mo · Heat stress mortality

Pain: Feed cost volatility, disease outbreaks, egg price swings

👷

MGNREGA Wages

₹8K–15K/mo · Monsoon-dependent

Pain: Payment delays, demand-supply mismatch, low wage rates

🌾

Agriculture

₹2–8L/season · Harvest lumps

Pain: Monsoon failure, input cost spikes, post-harvest price crashes

Built for Reality

Works where it matters

📶

Works without connectivity

Dexie.js + Workbox. Runs entirely on the device. Zero server dependency.

💬

Built for the people who do the work

Hindi/English, voice input, mobile-first. Designed for field officers, not software engineers.

📱

Enterprise & district views

Enterprise owner sees their data. DDM sees the district. Same engine, different viewports.

⚙️

Every threshold is configurable

50+ rules with research provenance. A NABARD economist can adjust without a developer.

50+

Configurable rules

Versioned, testable

17.4%

Use moneylenders

NAFIS 2022

5

Sector profiles

Dairy, retail, poultry, MGNREGA, agriculture

0

Servers required

Honest Constraints

What Drishti is and isn't

What Drishti IS

  • Explainable risk scoring — every flag has a reason
  • 5-layer household debt analysis
  • Offline-first PWA for rural connectivity
  • Configurable rulebook — no code changes for threshold updates
  • Data quality scoring — low confidence triggers field validation

What Drishti IS NOT

  • Not a loan decision engine — it informs, not decides
  • Not real-time DPI — signals are simulated in demo
  • Not a credit bureau replacement
  • Not production-ready — requires Supabase backend and real DPI integration
  • Not a replacement for field officer judgment

Current scope: 9 demo enterprises across 5 sectors. 479 DDM districts, 63M enterprises to reach.

See what your district looks like
when every enterprise is visible.

9 demo enterprises across 5 sectors. 50+ configurable rules. Every number explained. Zero setup.
